---
title: "Essay on Shiny-apps"
author: "Harrison Gan"
format: html
---

## What is a Shiny App?

A Shiny app is an interactive web application built with R. It allows users to interact with data, charts, and controls directly in a web browser.

## Main Components of a Shiny App

A Shiny app usually has two main parts:

-   **User Interface (UI):** what the user sees
-   **Server:** the part that processes the inputs and generates outputs

## Why Shiny Apps Are Useful

Shiny apps are useful because they allow people to explore data in a more engaging way than static charts. Users can filter data, change settings, and instantly see updated results.

## Example Workplace Use

If coworkers want to analyze sales performance, a Shiny app can help them choose a region, time period, or product category and instantly see updated graphs.

## Advantages

-   Interactive and user-friendly
-   Built in R
-   Good for dashboards and data exploration

## Limitations

-   Often requires a server for hosting
-   Can become slow with large or complex data

## Conclusion

Shiny apps are powerful tools for turning data into interactive experiences that help users better understand information and make decisions.
